content.description: Return value of av_malloc() is not checked for NULL after allocating s->edge_emu_buffer. If av_malloc fails, s->edge_emu_buffer is NULL and subsequent use at lines 2171 and 2185 dereferences the NULL pointer.
Added inner NULL check on the return value of av_malloc() for s->edge_emu_buffer. When allocation fails, set ret = AVERROR(ENOMEM) and goto error to safely abort decoding.

The outer condition 'if (!s->edge_emu_buffer)' existed before the fix to avoid redundant allocation, but it lacked an inner check on the av_malloc() return value. The fix preserves this outer guard and adds the missing NULL check inside.

content.summary: Missing NULL check on av_malloc return value for s->edge_emu_buffer leads to NULL pointer dereference in vp3_decode_frame
content.mechanism: In vp3_decode_frame(), when s->edge_emu_buffer is NULL, av_malloc() is called to allocate it. The return value of av_malloc() is never checked for NULL. If the allocation fails (e.g. out of memory), s->edge_emu_buffer remains NULL. Later, the same function uses s->edge_emu_buffer as a temp buffer pointer at lines 2171 and 2185, where it is passed to vp4_mc_loop_filter() and emulated_edge_mc() respectively, which dereference the NULL pointer, causing a segmentation fault or denial of service.

content.description: The CVE description and CWE-476 indicate NULL pointer dereference, but the root cause evidence from the patch is limited to one hunk. If there are additional allocation sites or code paths in vp3.c that also miss NULL checks, the fix might be incomplete.
